recording_notes: Phone filming Vizio monitor — same NEW STATION as V28. Red foam, moire/color-banding artifacts, close framing. ~304.5s task. Off-axis monitor capture limits exact mark visibility and lead-hand reads.

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments are visible and the trainee is orienting the needle and suture before committing to the first bite.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial setup is controlled, but there are a few extra regrasping movements before the needle is driven.

frame_analyses.description: The first bite is being placed through one marked side of the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle angle is acceptable and the entry point appears close to the target mark.

frame_analyses.description: The needle is exiting the opposite side and the trainee is pulling the suture through to complete placement.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pull-through is successful, though the drain rotates somewhat during extraction.

frame_analyses.description: The first knot throw is started with a bulky wrap pattern consistent with a surgeon's knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: This looks like a double throw, but it takes several seconds to seat flat.

frame_analyses.description: The trainee repositions the strands and begins the second throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Alternation appears to occur, but the lead instrument is only moderately clear from the camera angle.

frame_analyses.description: The second throw is being tightened down over the first.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The throw appears to be a single throw, though progress is slowed by repeated regrasping.

frame_analyses.description: The final throw is being laid and then gradually seated over the previous throws.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: This phase is the least efficient portion of the knot tying, with a prolonged effort to flatten and secure the knot.

frame_analyses.description: The knot is seated and the trainee transitions to handling the suture tails for trimming.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The knot appears intact and centered; no avulsion is seen.

frame_analyses.description: One tail is being isolated and the cutter is positioned near the knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Tail management is slow, with multiple adjustments before a clean cut.

frame_analyses.description: Final trimming is performed and short suture tails remain near the completed knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion occurs very near the end of the clip; both ends appear cut inside the trainer.

knot_assessments.notes: Initial throw appears bulky and consistent with a double throw; likely right-instrument led.
Second throw appears to be a single throw and likely alternates to the left instrument, though confidence is only moderate from this viewing angle.
Final throw appears to switch back and ultimately seats flat, but it takes the longest to tighten.

suture_placement.placement_notes: Needle placement looked reasonably close to the target marks; placement was not the major limiter of score.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No clear persistent gap, drain not avulsed, slit appeared reasonably approximated after the knot was seated.

technique_summary: The trainee completed the task within the 600-second limit and kept the Penrose drain mounted throughout, with needle placement appearing close to the marked targets. The major performance issue was efficiency: the second and third throws took a long time to seat, and the suture-cutting phase was especially prolonged because of repeated regrasping and tail management. The final knot appears centered and secure, with acceptable closure and no obvious slippage.
improvement_suggestions: Shorten the knot-tying time by committing earlier to each throw and reducing the number of intermediate regrasping maneuvers.
Make the left-right hand switch more deliberate and visually distinct between throws so the alternation is cleaner and faster.
For the cutting phase, isolate each tail before introducing the cutter so the final trimming can be done in fewer movements near the knot.
strengths: Needle passage appears close to both target marks and the drain was not avulsed during the task.
The final knot remained centered over the slit and appeared to stay secure after seating and trimming.
comparison_notes: Score agreement with Claude V29 within 4 FLS points (293 vs 297) despite very different penalty bookkeeping. Claude applied 8.0 total penalties (4.0 placement + 4.0 quality/efficiency); GPT-4o applied only 2.5 (placement only). The 4-point efficiency penalty is out-of-rubric — recurring pattern from V20/V22/V26. Both teachers identify cutting + throws 2/3 as the dominant loss centers and agree V29 is a fatigue/equipment-adaptation regression on the new Vizio station, not a skill regression. Largest confidence gap so far in the series: GPT-4o 0.64 vs Claude 0.30 — Claude penalizes the moire/color-banding artifacts much more heavily.
